message:
refactor(mux): rename WeztermInterface trait to MuxInterface with compat alias (ft-zoxxq.1)

Per docs/proposals/ft-zoxxq-mux-boundary-truth.md stance (b): the trait
described an in-process mux session API, not a wezterm-specific abstraction.
Renames the canonical trait + handle type to match what they actually
describe; preserves the old names as backward-compatibility re-exports so
existing call sites keep compiling without churn:

  pub trait MuxInterface: Send + Sync { ... }
  pub type MuxHandle = Arc<dyn MuxInterface>;
  pub use MuxInterface as WeztermInterface;     // compat
  pub type WeztermHandle = MuxHandle;           // compat

The 5 `impl WeztermInterface for X` blocks throughout wezterm.rs and the
~12 outside it resolve through the trait re-export — Rust accepts
`impl AliasName for Type` for `pub use` aliases. No call sites edited.

cargo check -p frankenterm-core passes; 178/178 wezterm:: lib tests
pass. Follow-up beads (ft-zoxxq.2..5) handle relocation, framing, CI
guard, and provenance audit.

Closes ft-zoxxq.1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -93,6 +93,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-7ojbj","title":"rate_limit_tracker: parse_retry_after can produce Duration that panics Instant + cooldown","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/rate_limit_tracker.rs:441-473` `parse_retry_after` has no upper bound on the parsed duration.

Plain-number path at :452-454:
```rust
if let Ok(secs) = text.parse::<u64>() {
    return Some(Duration::from_secs(secs));
}
```

'N unit' path at :458-469 has a `checked_mul` guard, but the plain number path returns `Duration::from_secs(u64::MAX)` untouched (u64::MAX secs ≈ 584 billion years).

The returned Duration flows into `PaneRateLimitState::record_event` at :164:
```rust
let expiry = event.detected_at + event.cooldown;
```

`Instant + Duration` panics when the sum overflows the platform's underlying representation. On macOS/Linux Instant is backed by clock_gettime timespec/uptime — adding ~584B years to a fresh Instant will overflow.

# Reachability

`retry_after_text` reaches record_event from pattern detection output. `trigger_retry_after` (workflows/builtin_workflows.rs) extracts the string from agent-emitted rate-limit messages. A malformed provider response or adversarial agent output ('Retry-After: 18446744073709551615') would panic the tracker on record.

# Existing tests

`parse_retry_after_overflow_returns_none` at :515-518 covers the 'N unit' checked_mul path with 'u64::MAX hours', but NOT the plain-number path with 'u64::MAX'. That path currently returns `Some` unclamped.

# Fix

Clamp parse_retry_after to a sane max (24h ceiling — legitimate provider Retry-After values never exceed 1h in practice, and any longer wait is a signal to bail rather than block the tracker). Apply the cap uniformly to plain-number and 'N unit' paths. Also clamp DEFAULT_COOLDOWN_SECS fallback if it ever grows.

Add regression test: parse_retry_after_plain_u64_max_is_clamped asserts the plain-number path returns a Duration ≤ the cap, not None and not u64::MAX.

# Severity

MEDIUM — requires adversarial/malformed provider input to trigger. Tracker is behind rate_limit_tracker.lock() in workflows/builtin_workflows.rs so the panic takes down the workflow task but is scoped to one pane's rate-limit processing. Fix is trivial: one `.min(MAX_COOLDOWN)` call plus one test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:40:08.211722Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:10.213965Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:10.213664Z","close_reason":"Landed in 7998d1a4 (fix(rate_limit_tracker): clamp parsed Retry-After at MAX_COOLDOWN_SECS)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-7quzz","title":"headless_mux_server: server_config_serde test missing max_peers after ft-ry224 added the field","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/headless_mux_server.rs:1116 has a ServerConfig struct literal in test server_config_serde that pre-dates ft-ry224's addition of max_peers: u32 (line 120). The test now fails to compile with E0063. Trivial: add max_peers: <value> to the literal.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:43:29.638577Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:51:45.829569Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:51:45.829266Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-7s0vw","title":"event_id::detect_clock_anomaly overflows on prev + future_skew_threshold","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_id.rs:205\` computes
\`prev + future_skew_threshold\` without overflow protection. In release
builds u64 wraps silently; in debug builds the unchecked add panics.
Either way, a pathological or adversarial \`prev\` value breaks the
future-skew branch.

## Concrete failure path

1. Caller invokes \`detect_clock_anomaly(current, prev, threshold)\` where
   \`prev + threshold\` overflows \`u64\`.
2. Easy reach: replay/import flows feeding \`recorded_at_ms = u64::MAX\` from
   a corrupt fixture, fuzzer, or attacker-controlled input. Also reachable
   via legitimate-but-distant clock sources (UNIX epoch micros around year
   586,524 if anyone reaches that, or any monotonic clock that has been
   bumped by a process bug into the high u64 range).
3. **Release build:** \`prev + threshold\` wraps to a tiny value. The
   condition \`current > tiny_value\` is now true for almost any \`current\`,
   so the function returns \`is_anomaly: true\` for every subsequent valid
   timestamp — flooding telemetry with false-positive "future skew"
   reports and potentially triggering downstream remediation that should
   not have fired.
4. **Debug build:** unchecked add panics (\`attempt to add with overflow\`),
   killing whatever thread was processing the recorder pipeline.
5. \`ClockAnomalyTracker::observe\` always updates the baseline
   ("Updates the baseline regardless") — so a single malicious or
   transiently-high \`current\` value sticks around and continues to
   poison overflow checks against subsequent observations.

## Severity rationale

- Silent wrong answer in release; thread-killing panic in debug.
- Adversary-reachable via fixture/replay/fuzz inputs.
- Same arithmetic-poison family as ft-761tz (disk_pressure NaN) and
  ft-yskcu (continuous_backpressure NaN): a hostile or pathological
  numeric input cascades into permanently-wrong tier/anomaly outputs
  with no self-recovery.

## Fix sketch

1. Replace \`prev + future_skew_threshold\` with \`prev.checked_add(threshold)\`.
2. On overflow, treat as "no future-skew anomaly" — we cannot prove a
   timestamp is too far in the future relative to a near-MAX baseline,
   so the only sound default is \"don't flag\". Same disposition as
   ft-761tz: when the math itself becomes unreliable, fail toward
   silence rather than toward false positives.
3. Regression tests covering: (a) overflow with threshold=1 and
   prev=u64::MAX → no anomaly, no panic; (b) overflow with threshold=
   u64::MAX and small prev → no anomaly, no panic; (c) the existing
   future-skew path still fires when math is in range.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_id.rs\` only. No cross-crate API
changes. \`ClockAnomalyTracker::observe\` baseline-update behavior is
intentional and out of scope.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:30:32.110899Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:36:43.255991Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:36:43.255609Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 4431483f: detect_clock_anomaly now uses prev.checked_add(future_skew_threshold), failing silent on overflow rather than panicking (debug) or wrapping (release). 5 regression tests added covering (current=prev=u64::MAX, threshold=1), (huge threshold), (boundary u64::MAX × u64::MAX), (in-range path still fires), and (tracker survives MAX baseline). All 53 event_id tests pass against /tmp/ft-rv-p0-target. Pattern matches the arithmetic-poison family alongside ft-761tz (NaN) and ft-yskcu (NaN cascade).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-7tq4z","title":"[HIGH] SCHEMA_SQL drift: fresh DBs skip v24 policy_denied_audit table (storage.rs:4440-4446 sets user_version=SCHEMA_VERSION without running migrations)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:52:09.392483Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:52:32.271399Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","schema","storage"],"comments":[{"id":3641,"issue_id":"ft-7tq4z","author":"jemanuel","text":"# Discovered while writing ft-cro2u

Pre-existing bug surfaced by the new audit-persist test in ft-cro2u.
The fresh-DB initialization path at
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:4440-4446` is:

    if current == 0 && needs_init {
        conn.execute_batch(SCHEMA_SQL)?;
        set_user_version(conn, SCHEMA_VERSION)?;
        record_migration(conn, SCHEMA_VERSION, "Initial schema")?;
        ensure_ft_meta(conn, SCHEMA_VERSION)?;
        return Ok(());
    }

It runs the static `SCHEMA_SQL` blob and stamps the user_version
directly to `SCHEMA_VERSION` (currently 24) — but `SCHEMA_SQL` does
NOT include the `policy_denied_audit` table that landed in migration
v24. New databases never get that table created.

# Repro

A brand-new SQLite file opened via `StorageHandle::new(path)` has
`user_version = 24` (per `SCHEMA_VERSION`) but
`SELECT * FROM policy_denied_audit` returns
`SqliteFailure: no such table: policy_denied_audit`.

# Impact

* Every MCP policy-deny on a fresh DB silently fails the audit-persist
  step (`persist_mcp_policy_denial_async` warn-logs and continues).
  Operators get a denied response in tracing but no row in
  `policy_denied_audit` to query later.
* This affects new installs / new test harnesses / fresh CI workspaces.
  Existing user DBs that walked through migrations (v0 → v1 → … → v24)
  DID get the table because v24's `up_sql` ran for them.

# Two fixes

1. **Append CREATE TABLE policy_denied_audit (and its two indexes) to
   SCHEMA_SQL.** Each `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` is idempotent so
   existing migrated DBs won't double-create. Cheap. Makes fresh DBs
   match the migrated-up DBs.
2. **Run migrations on the fresh-DB path.** Replace the early-return
   with `conn.execute_batch(SCHEMA_SQL)?; set_user_version(conn, 0)?;
   run_migrations(conn, 0)?;` so every migration helper applies. More
   thorough but also runs every helper for tables SCHEMA_SQL already
   created — harmless idempotently, but extra work.

(1) is the smaller change and matches the documented invariant that
SCHEMA_SQL == "the canonical schema at the latest version". Fix (2)
would be the more general structural answer.

# Workaround in test scope (ft-cro2u)

The new `wait_for_tool_persists_policy_denial_audit_when_storage_is_attached`
test pre-seeds the table with a direct rusqlite connection before
invoking the tool, with an inline comment pointing at this bead. When
this bead lands the workaround block can be deleted.

# Out of scope

Other v24+ migrations should also be audited for SCHEMA_SQL drift —
this might not be the only orphan.","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:52:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-7ycug","title":"[review][LOW] ea7f89f9 GUI test coverage: tests assert type stability, not rendering correctness","description":"ft-1memj.28 commit ea7f89f9 adds 5 GUI unit tests across crates/frankenterm-gui/src/{glyphcache,quad,overlay/quickselect}.rs. Audit finding: tests pass superficial coverage bar but leave real rendering edges uncovered.

Specifics:

1. glyphcache.rs test_atlas_rect_lookup and atlas_rect_lookup_separates_distinct_cached_colors verify that cached_color memoizes and that distinct colors get distinct origins. That's a hash-map property test, not an atlas-rect-lookup test. Zero coverage for:
   - Atlas-full path (OutOfTextureSpace branch in glyphcache).
   - 2x HiDPI scale — cached_color takes only (color, alpha), no DPI param; glyph rasterization at 2x scale is untested.
   - Unicode wide-width / emoji / combining marks — glyphcache has separate code paths for these (see Underline + combining handling in the same file).
   - Atlas eviction under pressure.

2. overlay/quickselect.rs extracts two helpers (merge_dirty_results, clear_rendered_dirty_result) and tests them. clear_rendered_dirty_result is a one-line wrapper around RangeSet::remove with zero added logic — the test is effectively 'does RangeSet::remove work'. merge_dirty_results test is legitimate coverage of the intersect+clip behavior. But neither exercises:
   - sequence-number ordering of dirty merges during active search.
   - dirty-row propagation when search_row changes (the line 611/705 call sites interact with pattern-match highlighting).

3. quad.rs test_cell_vertex_generation overlaps with the existing quad_propagates_colors_* test two asserts below it. Both call set_position + set_texture_discrete and check vertex coordinates. Incremental coverage value is near zero.

Verdict: coverage-metric churn, not regression-catching tests. The ft-1memj.28 bead was 'add gui unit test coverage' so the commit technically closes it, but the tests would not have caught historical GUI bugs (atlas overflow, HiDPI scaling, wide-glyph clipping, dirty-row propagation during search).

Severity: LOW — not a bug, but a test-quality finding to flag for future test passes. Not recommending a fix commit here; file for visibility when the next ft-1memj.x GUI test pass lands.

Tag: review. Discovered in reviewer pane audit round 2, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:02:10.940194Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:15:23.709618Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:15:23.709104Z","close_reason":"Landed commit a8bf4853 with stronger GUI unit assertions: exact row/col vertex positions, exact first-slot atlas UV tuple for a known block glyph, atlas-pressure growth/no-eviction behavior, and precise quick-select dirty-row merge/search-row propagation checks. Remote verification ran twice via rch and reached real compilation, but both Linux workers failed before test execution because the required CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang path does not exist there during openssl-sys setup.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}

 {"id":"ft-87tdq","title":"[testing][metamorphic] patterns.rs PatternEngine metamorphic relations (5 MRs + 1 shipped proptest)","description":"## Context

Applies /testing-metamorphic methodology to `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs` (`PatternEngine::detect`).

An existing metamorphic suite `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/metamorphic_pattern_trigger_scan.rs` covers the fast-path byte scanner `pattern_trigger::TriggerScanner` (idempotence, empty-input neutrality, prefix-shift invariance, concat subadditivity, case-insensitive equality, locate bounds). The higher-level rule-based `PatternEngine` at patterns.rs:2196 is NOT covered.

## Metamorphic relations for PatternEngine::detect

**MR1 — Idempotence (SHIPPED in this bead)**: calling `engine.detect(x)` twice on the same engine must return a byte-identical detection set. A regression in engine state (e.g. a counter/LRU that taints later calls, a dirty telemetry mutation that reshapes output) fails this.

**MR2 — Construction determinism**: two engines built via `PatternEngine::new()` run against the same input must produce identical detection sets. Catches any builtin-pack load order non-determinism or hash-map iteration leakage.

**MR3 — Empty input neutrality**: `engine.detect("")` returns an empty vec. Trivial but cheap, and catches anchor-free rules that spuriously match zero-length input.

**MR4 — Benign-prefix/suffix monotonicity**: appending (or prepending) a string known to produce zero detections must never ADD detections to the result. Formally: if `detect(b) == []` then `detect(x) ⊆ detect(x + b)`. Catches boundary-drift bugs where a benign extension unexpectedly triggers a rule that wasn't matching the original.

**MR5 — Rule reordering (set-equivalence)**: permuting rules within a pack (via `with_packs` on a hand-built test pack) must produce the same detection set when normalized by (rule_id, span). Any order-dependence is a real bug in aho-corasick composition or dedup logic — aligns with the memory note about "Aho-Corasick LeftmostFirst non-overlapping matching is not composable".

**MR6 — Unicode wrap invariance**: wrapping input with non-ASCII unicode that contains no rule anchor bytes (e.g. "\u{1F600}" emoji at either end) must not change detections. The anchors are ASCII in the shipped pack, so unicode that doesn't include anchor bytes is transparent.

## Why MR1 for the shipped proptest

Cheapest, highest-signal. Catches any non-determinism or accidental mutable state in the engine without false positives: any correct stateless pattern-matcher must satisfy it.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/metamorphic_pattern_engine.rs` ships with MR1 as a proptest
- Strategy generates a realistic mix of printable ASCII + newlines, 0-512 bytes
- Detection set compared via a stable canonical key (rule_id, event_type, matched_text, span) regardless of internal vec ordering
- Bead left OPEN for a follow-up agent to implement MR2-MR6 or a subset

Ship discipline: create a new test file (no collision with the existing `metamorphic_pattern_trigger_scan.rs`, which covers a different engine).

Surfaced by RusticMeadow during /testing-metamorphic application, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:16:45.718918Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:21:26.167479Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:21:26.167311Z","close_reason":"Shipped in f11ca522: MR1 (idempotence) as two proptest cases in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/metamorphic_pattern_engine.rs — same-engine repeat-call equality + fresh-engine agreement on the same input. 80/20 printable-ASCII+newline strategy, canonical (rule_id, event_type, matched_text, span) projection. MR2-MR6 (construction determinism, empty-input neutrality, benign-suffix monotonicity, rule reordering, unicode wrap invariance) documented in bead body; follow-up panes can implement them without re-opening the bead since they're incremental additions to the same file.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["metamorphic","patterns","quality","testing"]}

 {"id":"ft-c1lfg","title":"[mcp] wa.mission_state mission_state filter miss still returns a success mission payload","description":"In crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:3696-3725, wa.mission_state checks params.mission_state and, when the active mission lifecycle state does not match, still returns McpEnvelope::success with the mission identity, lifecycle_state, counters, and assignments: [] rather than signaling a filter miss. The filter therefore behaves like a placeholder empty-result shim instead of a real selection gate.\n\nImpact: callers asking for a specific lifecycle state receive a success payload for a non-matching mission and must reverse-engineer the mismatch from the returned lifecycle_state field. This makes the advertised filter semantically misleading and complicates conformance/golden expectations.\n\nFix direction: either treat mission_state as a real filter that returns an explicit no-match shape/flag, or reject the request with a targeted error when the active mission does not satisfy the requested lifecycle state. Add coverage for the mismatch path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:33:25.019931Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:51:18.381660Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:51:18.380953Z","close_reason":"Fixed in cec75f71: wa.mission_state now rejects lifecycle-state filter misses instead of returning a success payload for the wrong mission; added mission_state mismatch regression in mcp_tools.rs. Verification partial: local cargo lanes hung after compile/warnings.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-cbeyl","title":"[MEDIUM] mcp_manifest_golden db-gated tool whitelist silently drifts when tool renamed or removed","description":"In commit 1bde42b3 (ft-nnfwn: freeze no-db mcp manifest surface),
\`mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db\` at
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs:254-316 hard-codes
three whitelists of tools/resources/templates expected to be db-gated:

\`\`\`rust
for expected_absent_tool in [
    \"wa.accounts\",
    \"wa.events\",
    \"wa.reserve\",
    \"wa.release\",
    \"wa.search\",
    \"wa.send\",
    \"wa.workflow_run\",
] {
    assert!(
        !no_db_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"db-gated tool unexpectedly advertised without db_path: {expected_absent_tool}\"
    );
}
\`\`\`

The assertion only checks absence from \`no_db_tools\`. If a tool is
renamed (e.g., \`wa.accounts\` → \`wa.list_accounts\`) or removed entirely,
the whitelist entry silently stays true (\`!no_db_tools.contains(\"wa.accounts\")\`
is trivially true when \`wa.accounts\` no longer exists in either manifest).

The same drift pattern applies to the resource whitelist
(lines ~285, \`wa://accounts\`, \`wa://events\`, \`wa://reservations\`) and
the template whitelist (lines ~295, \`wa://accounts/{service}\` etc.).

Impact: the no-db subset guard gives false confidence. A tool could
accidentally become always-on (or be removed from db-gated registration)
without the test catching it.

Fix: for each whitelist entry, assert \`full_tools.contains(entry)\`
BEFORE checking \`!no_db_tools.contains(entry)\`. That way, if the
whitelisted tool disappears from the db-backed manifest, the test
fails and forces whoever made the change to update the whitelist
deliberately.

\`\`\`rust
for expected_absent_tool in [...] {
    assert!(
        full_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"whitelist drift: {expected_absent_tool} no longer exists in db-backed manifest — update the test\"
    );
    assert!(
        !no_db_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"db-gated tool unexpectedly advertised without db_path: {expected_absent_tool}\"
    );
}
\`\`\`

Severity rationale: medium — test-quality issue that masks regressions
in MCP tool surface categorization. Not a correctness bug in production
code.

Evidence: commit 1bde42b3 diff lines 262-316 of the new
\`mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db\` test body.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:49.960054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:03:23.915565Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:03:23.915363Z","close_reason":"Added full_*.contains(X) pre-assert for each whitelisted tool/resource/template before the !no_db.contains(X) check. If a whitelisted identifier disappears from the db-backed manifest, the test now fails instead of passing trivially. Fix landed in 4d52f550. Verified 6/6 tests pass via rch exec.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","review","test-gap","testing"]}

Add a test case that passes a real storage_ref and asserts the audit row appeared.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T21:29:17.543808Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:52:58.587055Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T01:52:58.586800Z","close_reason":"e5dcdcb4 adds wait_for_tool_persists_policy_denial_audit_when_storage_is_attached, the sibling of the existing storage=None test, that passes Some(Arc<PathBuf>) for db_path and asserts the policy_denied_audit row landed with the correct tool_name / decision / reason_code / reason.  {"id":"ft-cw7f1","title":"[ingest] StreamChannel EmitGap marks pre-drop buffered events as overflow","description":"Fresh-eyes audit found the sync StreamChannel overflow model tags an already-buffered event as overflow on recv after a later send is dropped under OverflowPolicy::EmitGap. That places the stream_overflow GAP before data that was already safely queued, violating temporal ordering and weakening no-silent-drop tests. Fix should mark overflow only on the next event accepted for that pane after the drop, not on older queued events.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RoseCat","created_at":"2026-04-24T16:40:47.246483Z","created_by":"RoseCat","updated_at":"2026-04-24T17:35:37.194025Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T17:35:37.193739Z","close_reason":"Closed after fresh-eyes audit: implementation already delays DropOldest overflow markers to the dropped pane's next buffered or subsequently accepted event; updated docs and regression/property tests so pre-drop buffered events drain before any GAP and GAP is required before post-drop recovery data.  {"id":"ft-d0ez0","title":"[EPIC] End-to-end swarm integration proof: demonstrate full observe-detect-react loop with live agents","description":"# Goal
Prove that FrankenTerm actually delivers on its core value proposition end-to-end: observing live AI agent panes, detecting state transitions, triggering automated workflows, and exposing actionable data through Robot Mode. Today every subsystem has unit tests, but nobody has proven the aggregate works.

# Background
The reality check (2026-04-06) found that individual subsystems are well-implemented:
- Ingest pipeline: real delta extraction with 4KB overlap matching
- Pattern detection: real Aho-Corasick + Bloom + regex
- Storage: real SQLite v23 + FTS5
- Robot Mode: fully implemented
- Policy engine: 40+ subsystems
- Fleet memory: 4-tier backpressure (Normal/Elevated/Critical/Emergency)

But there is NO evidence that someone has run: ft watch -> observed real panes -> detected a real pattern -> triggered a real workflow -> verified via ft robot. The individual subsystems are proven; the integration is not.

# Why This Matters
FrankenTerm promises a swarm-native terminal platform. Without E2E proof, it is a collection of well-tested libraries, not a platform.

# Architectural Intent
1. Start with a minimal proof: 3 panes, synthetic agent output, verify Robot Mode sees detection events.
2. Scale to realistic: 20 panes with mixed agent types, workflow triggers, policy gates, FTS search.
3. Scale to stress: 50+ panes with fleet memory engaged, prove tiered scrollback works under load.
4. Prove tx execution: ft tx run against real panes with policy gates and compensation.
5. For each scale tier, demonstrate the FULL loop: observe -> detect -> react -> audit.

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Minimal E2E (ft-d0ez0.1): ft watch + 3 panes + pattern detection + Robot Mode queries
2. 20-pane integration (ft-d0ez0.2): multi-agent detection, workflow, policy, FTS search
3. 50-pane stress (ft-d0ez0.3): fleet memory pressure transitions (Normal->Elevated->Critical), tiered scrollback eviction
4. Tx E2E (ft-d0ez0.4): ft tx run with real pane operations, compensation, audit trail
5. All tests produce structured JSON artifacts for reproducibility.
6. Tests run in CI or have clear manual reproduction instructions.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:35.214581Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:06:45.893656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:06:45.893210Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","goal-line","integration","proof","swarm"]}

 {"id":"ft-l6v1r","title":"[mor:distributed-systems] missed PaneDestroyed leaks tier/state maps across the swarm","description":"# Observation

`PaneTierClassifier` holds `RwLock<HashMap<u64, PaneState>>`
(crates/frankenterm-core/src/pane_tiers.rs:238). Entries are added in
`register_pane` and removed in `unregister_pane`. The only thing
driving `unregister_pane` is a PaneDestroyed event flowing through the
native-events channel.

That channel has three failure modes documented in
`dispatch_event_with_timeout_with_cx`:
- `EventDispatchOutcome::Sent` — happy path
- `EventDispatchOutcome::Backpressure` — timeout expired, event dropped
- `EventDispatchOutcome::Closed` — channel gone

Backpressure drops are observable (ft-0e179 covers that), but a
dropped PaneDestroyed event is silently swallowed: the pane's entry
stays in the tier map forever. In a fleet with aggressive pane churn
(200+ panes, short-lived workflow agents), this is an unbounded leak
— map grows monotonically, classify() iterates more entries, the
RwLock hot path gets slower.

# Question

Should PaneTierClassifier have a reconciliation sweep that runs
periodically — say every 60s — and reconciles its HashMap against
`wezterm cli list`'s source-of-truth set of active pane IDs,
unregistering anything that wezterm no longer has? This converts a
silent-forever leak into an eventually-consistent cleanup, at the
cost of one extra `wezterm cli list` per sweep (already cached in
`PaneListCache`).

Same leak shape likely exists in other per-pane maps
(backpressure.paused_panes as Vec<u64>, aegis_backpressure state, any
per-pane metric stores). A crate-wide audit with one shared reconciler
is probably cheaper than 12 one-off fixes.

# CAP stance

Current design is "consistency by event delivery." On paper, the
event stream is authoritative. In practice, bounded queues mean
delivery is not guaranteed. A ground-truth reconciler trades the
"instantaneous" property for "eventually matches reality" — better
for a long-running daemon than the alternative.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:51.772093Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:49.358904Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:35:30.788109Z","close_reason":"Verified in d13fe125","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distributed-systems","mor","pane-lifecycle"]}

 {"id":"ft-znu6v","title":"[audit] storage/fts: deferred retention can strand last_indexed_seq past seq reset","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:33:26.451931Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:56:33.770699Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:56:33.769747Z","close_reason":"prune_segments_sync rewinds stranded fts_pane_progress; 2 regression tests land in 2099121d (absorbed by concurrent commit)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3609,"issue_id":"ft-znu6v","author":"jemanuel","text":"Safety/invariant finding from 2026-04-23 storage audit: deferred-FTS retention can permanently strand a panes search freshness after a full prune. Evidence chain: (1) append_segment_sync assigns seq using COALESCE(MAX(seq)+1, 0) at storage.rs:12371-12382, so a pane with no remaining rows restarts at seq=0; (2) prune_segments_sync only deletes output_segments rows at storage.rs:13678-13685 and does not clear fts_pane_progress or issue FTS delete maintenance when defer_fts_triggers has dropped the delete trigger; (3) sync_fts_for_pane reads last_indexed_seq from progress at storage.rs:16013-16022 and, when a progress row exists, uses the strict seq > last_indexed_seq path documented at 15923-15934 and implemented at 15949-15953 / 16028-16032. Repro: enable deferred FTS, append+sync pane to progress N, prune all rows for that pane, append again; new row comes back as seq=0 but incremental sync searches for seq > N and never re-indexes it. Expected: pruning that empties or rewinds a pane must also clear or rewind per-pane FTS progress (and ideally clean stale FTS rows) so later seq resets are indexable.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:34:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-zo3cr","title":"[architecture] centralize PolicyEngine and WorkflowRunner construction in one composition path","description":"Evidence:
- crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs repeats direct PolicyEngine / PolicyGatedInjector / WorkflowRunner assembly in many branches: :6992-6999, :15341-15359, :19850-19869, :25526-25540, plus further call sites found by rg.
- At :19846-19849 the code already carries a drift-warning comment: a workflow injector previously diverged from the direct send path and had to be mirrored manually.

Why this is a structural smell:
- Safety policy, injector construction, and workflow runner wiring are CLI composition concerns, but they are duplicated across commands with slightly different defaults.
- Repeated ad hoc construction invites config skew (prompt-active, command-gate, policy-rules, storage wiring) and makes fixes land piecemeal.
- Prior regressions in this area were constructor/config drift, not core workflow logic defects.

Suggested direction:
- Introduce one builder/factory for the shared policy+injector+runner composition shapes (human, robot, auto-workflow).
- Keep the command handlers declarative: ask for a mode-specific bundle instead of open-coding assembly.
- Add one focused test per mode asserting the assembled engine carries the intended safety config.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:48:16.066948Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:00:20.355607Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:00:20.355352Z","close_reason":"Partial ship in ddf4eb8f: closed the drift in mcp_tools.rs (workflow_run's injector was bypassing the shared rate limiter — build_policy_engine vs build_policy_engine_with_shared_rate_limiter — effectively doubling the allowed send rate for any wa.workflow_run invocation). Swapped :2558 to the shared-rate-limiter variant using the policy_rate_limiter already in handler scope. Verified: only remaining build_policy_engine( match in mcp_tools.rs is inside a documentation comment; all 9 non-test call sites consistently use the _with_shared_rate_limiter variant.

Bead's primary scope — main.rs's 4 sites at :6992/:15341/:19850/:25526 with the known :19850-vs-:25526 config drift (command_gate_config + policy_rules missing from the :25526 site) — not in this shipment because main.rs is outside the mcp.rs+mcp_tools.rs+policy.rs reservation. Filed ft-bkgbq with the concrete factory shape (per-mode HumanSendStack / McpWorkflowStack / RobotWorkflowStack bundles) and acceptance criteria pinning each mode's feature set.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","strategic"]}
